topics.name: Backend Web Development
topics.description: A structured learning path for becoming a backend web developer.

topics.name: How the Internet Works
topics.description: The TCP/IP stack, packet routing, DNS lookups, and request journey from browser to server and back.

topics.name: HTTP and HTTPS
topics.description: Methods, status codes, headers, request/response lifecycle, and differences between HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, and HTTP/3.

topics.name: Browsers
topics.description: What a browser does with a response, including parsing, rendering, and the request/response cycle.

topics.name: Hosting
topics.description: DNS, domain registration, TLS certificates, and what a hosting provider provides.

topics.name: Syntax and Control Flow
topics.description: Variables, conditionals, loops, functions, scoping rules

topics.name: Data Structures
topics.description: Arrays, lists, sets, maps/dictionaries, stacks, queues, trees

topics.name: Object-Oriented Programming
topics.description: Classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, encapsulation

topics.name: Functional Programming Concepts
topics.description: Immutability, pure functions, map/filter/reduce, higher-order functions

topics.name: Error Handling
topics.description: Exceptions, error returns, when to catch versus when to propagate

topics.name: Git
topics.description: Git is a version control system that includes branches, commits, merges, rebases, and pull requests.

topics.name: Remote Repositories
topics.description: Remote repositories include GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket, and involve pushing, pulling, and code review workflows.

topics.name: Workflows
topics.description: Workflows include trunk-based development, Git Flow, and feature branches, and involve understanding which workflow fits which team size.

topics.name: Relational Databases
topics.description: SQL fundamentals, PostgreSQL, and MySQL

topics.name: NoSQL Databases
topics.description: Key-value stores, document stores, wide-column stores, and graph databases

topics.name: Database Design
topics.description: Normalization, primary keys, foreign keys, indexes, and query plans

topics.name: ORMs
topics.description: Object-relational mappers like SQLAlchemy, Hibernate, and Sequelize

topics.name: Transactions
topics.description: ACID properties, isolation levels, deadlocks, and locking mechanisms

topics.name: REST
topics.description: Resource-oriented design, HTTP verbs, status codes, and content negotiation.

topics.name: GraphQL
topics.description: Schema definition, queries, mutations, resolvers, and the n+1 problem.

topics.name: gRPC
topics.description: Protocol buffers, RPC semantics, streaming, and binary protocols.

topics.name: API Documentation
topics.description: OpenAPI/Swagger, generating client libraries, and keeping docs in sync with code.

topics.name: Authentication Patterns
topics.description: Methods for verifying user identity, including session-based, token-based, and OAuth 2.0.

topics.name: Password Storage
topics.description: Secure methods for storing passwords, such as hashing with bcrypt or argon2.

topics.name: Authorization Models
topics.description: Models for controlling access, including role-based and attribute-based access control.

topics.name: JSON Web Tokens (JWT)
topics.description: A token-based authentication method.

topics.name: OAuth 2.0
topics.description: An authorization framework.

topics.name: OpenID Connect
topics.description: An authentication protocol.

topics.name: API Keys
topics.description: A method for authenticating API requests.

topics.name: Mutual TLS
topics.description: A method for authenticating API requests using TLS.

topics.name: Bcrypt
topics.description: A password hashing algorithm.

topics.name: Argon2
topics.description: A password hashing algorithm.

topics.name: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
topics.description: A model for controlling access based on user roles.

topics.name: Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)
topics.description: A model for controlling access based on user attributes.

topics.name: In-Memory Caches
topics.description: Using tools like Redis and Memcached for caching, including TTLs and eviction policies.

topics.name: CDN Caching
topics.description: Using edge caching, cache headers, and surrogate keys for caching.

topics.name: Application-Level Caching
topics.description: Using memoization, query result caches, and cache-aside and read-through patterns for caching.

topics.name: Redis
topics.description: An in-memory data store used for caching.

topics.name: Memcached
topics.description: A high-performance caching system.

topics.name: TTLs
topics.description: Time-to-live values for cache expiration.

topics.name: Eviction Policies
topics.description: Strategies for removing items from a cache.

topics.name: Cache Invalidation
topics.description: Strategies for updating cache contents.

topics.name: Edge Caching
topics.description: Caching at the edge of a network.

topics.name: Cache Headers
topics.description: HTTP headers for controlling cache behavior.

topics.name: Surrogate Keys
topics.description: Keys used to identify and manage cache contents.

topics.name: Memoization
topics.description: Caching the results of expensive function calls.

topics.name: Query Result Caches
topics.description: Caching the results of database queries.

topics.name: Cache-Aside
topics.description: A caching pattern that loads data into a cache on demand.

topics.name: Read-Through
topics.description: A caching pattern that loads data into a cache on read operations.

topics.name: Unit Testing
topics.description: Testing individual functions in isolation using mocking and faking external dependencies.

topics.name: Integration Testing
topics.description: Testing how components work together, including database, cache, and external services.

topics.name: End-to-End Testing
topics.description: Exercising the full system through its public API.

topics.name: Test Doubles
topics.description: Using stubs, mocks, fakes, and spies in testing, and knowing when to use each.

topics.name: Property-Based Testing
topics.description: Generating inputs to find edge cases that example-based tests miss.

topics.name: Docker
topics.description: Docker images, containers, Dockerfiles, layers, multi-stage builds

topics.name: Container Orchestration
topics.description: Kubernetes, ECS, Nomad, pods, deployments, services, ingress

topics.name: CI/CD Pipelines
topics.description: Continuous integration with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, build, test, deploy stages

topics.name: Cloud Providers
topics.description: AWS, GCP, Azure, core compute, storage, and networking primitives

topics.name: Structured Logging
topics.description: Using JSON logs over freeform strings, including log levels and correlation IDs.

topics.name: Metrics
topics.description: Using Prometheus, StatsD, OpenTelemetry for counters, gauges, histograms, and measuring methods.

topics.name: Distributed Tracing
topics.description: Using spans, traces, and propagation across service boundaries.

topics.name: Alerting
topics.description: Using SLOs, error budgets, and determining when to page or ticket.

topics.name: Horizontal vs Vertical Scaling
topics.description: Deciding when to add machines versus making them bigger for scalability

topics.name: Load Balancing
topics.description: Techniques like round-robin, least-connections, and consistent hashing for distributing load

topics.name: Message Queues
topics.description: Using queues like Kafka, RabbitMQ, or SQS to introduce asynchrony

topics.name: Failure Modes
topics.description: Handling failures with circuit breakers, retries, timeouts, bulkheads, and degradation

topics.name: Disaster Recovery
topics.description: Planning for backups, restore drills, and RTO/RPO targets

topics.name: Common Vulnerabilities
topics.description: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, SSRF, and the OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

topics.name: Transport Security
topics.description: TLS, certificate management, and HSTS for secure data transfer.

topics.name: Secrets Management
topics.description: Managing secrets using environment variables or secret managers with rotation and least privilege principle.

topics.name: Dependency Security
topics.description: Scanning for known vulnerabilities, supply-chain attacks, and lockfile discipline for secure dependencies.

relationships.rationale: Object-Oriented Programming builds upon foundational programming concepts such as syntax and control flow.
Both Object-Oriented and Functional Programming Concepts are programming paradigms.
RESTful APIs often use HTTP or HTTPS protocols.
API Documentation is crucial for understanding and using RESTful APIs.
Backend Web Development often involves deploying applications to hosting services.
Understanding Git is necessary for working with Remote Repositories.
Database Design principles apply to Relational Databases.
Object-Relational Mappers (ORMs) are often used in conjunction with well-designed databases.
Authentication Patterns and Authorization Models are both crucial for security.
Unit Testing is often a precursor to Integration Testing.
Understanding Docker is necessary for Container Orchestration.
Load Balancing is a technique used in both Horizontal and Vertical Scaling.

validation_events.message: Section 'Backend Web Development Roadmap' extracted 1 topics
